content: Performance concern raised about the inbox architecture. Current structure (one JSONL file per session, processed flag per line) requires scanning all session files and filtering within them. Proposed alternatives: (1) one file per message with atomic pickup-and-delete, (2) separate pending/ and done/ directories with file moves on completion, (3) append-only log with offset tracking. Direction not yet chosen.

provenance.raw_agent: Some alternative approaches that would scale better: One file per message, Separate pending/ and done/ directories, Append-only log + offset tracking. Which direction were you thinking of taking this?
provenance.raw_user: If they are being processed by another system, isn't that going to cause a performance degradation?
